steps.name: idempotency-key-contract
steps.summary: Publish job keys now deterministically bind job_type, post_version_id, and content_hash. PostgreSQL persistence returns the existing job for duplicate logical requests while changed versions or content hashes create distinct keys.

steps.name: persistent-job-lock-and-retry-stop
steps.summary: PostgreSQL-backed five-minute job leases now allow only the current lease owner to finish a job, reclaim expired work safely, and stop retryable stages after the same failure repeats twice with a durable usage_events row and operator-alert result.

steps.name: usage-events-cost-ledger
steps.summary: The shared PostgreSQL usage_events ledger now records source fetch, LLM, search embedding, IndexNow, and Discord usage with provider/model/token/cost/status fields. Daily and monthly budget guards block new costly work as budget_exceeded, while real providers and production activation remain disabled.

steps.name: privacy-scanner-and-redaction
steps.summary: A shared fail-closed scanner now blocks credentials, internal network details, personal contacts, configured organization names, and configured private repository names before LLM generation, after writer output, and at the PostgreSQL public-read boundary. Audit messages retain only finding categories with redacted values.
